triggers.rationale: Demand for Coffee is rising in France (momentum 11.98, +485%). Top origins: Brazil (40%, -28%), Germany (15%, -4%), Vietnam (15%, +7%), Mexico (6%, -24%), Indonesia (6%, -13%). insights.headline: PROCURE: Coffee — source from Spain (emerging origin)
insights.narrative:
**Increase coffee procurement from Vietnam while selectively trialling Spain, Turkey, and Morocco as emerging complementary origins.**

Vietnam is the only top-five origin showing positive supply growth (+0.0721) and holds a meaningful 14.9% import share, making it the most defensible primary source given current supply dynamics — notably, competitor Rewe is already positioned there, underscoring its relevance. Demand signals from Wikipedia show strong momentum (11.98), growth (4.85), and accelerating interest (5.16) for coffee in France, justifying volume expansion. Spain, Turkey, and Morocco are flagged as emerging recommended origins and warrant small-scale trial procurement to diversify away from declining sources such as Brazil (−0.28 growth, despite its dominant 39.8% share) and Mexico (−0.24 growth), reducing concentration risk without overcommitting ahead of validated supply performance.

insights.headline: WATCH: Knitwear & Apparel — source from Denmark (emerging origin)
insights.narrative: **Prioritise sourcing Knitwear & Apparel for the Netherlands market from Denmark, supplemented by Morocco and/or Austria, to establish clear differentiation from competitors.** Demand signals are strong — Wikipedia momentum stands at 0.59 with acceleration of 0.84 — while all established origins (China, Vietnam, Bangladesh, Cambodia, Indonesia) are in volume decline, with growth rates ranging from -0.15 to -0.34, undermining their reliability as primary sources. All six benchmarked competitors source from Bangladesh or Turkey, so pivoting to the recommended emerging origins (Denmark, Morocco, Austria) directly avoids origin commoditisation and positions Lidl's knitwear offering as distinctively European in provenance.

insights.headline: PROCURE: Drinkware & Tumblers — source from Germany (emerging origin)
insights.narrative: **Prioritise sourcing Drinkware & Tumblers from Germany as the primary emerging origin, while selectively developing Netherlands and Poland as complementary suppliers.** Demand signals show strong positive momentum (1.06) with accelerating growth (acceleration: 0.30) on Wikipedia, supporting a proactive procurement stance. Germany offers a growing supply share (29.9% growth) with greater supply chain proximity and reliability, while Netherlands (148.5% growth) and Poland (119.1% growth) present high-growth alternatives that reduce over-dependence on China, which currently dominates at 95.1% share but is showing a decline of -5.3% — the same single-origin risk Tesco appears exposed to.

insights.headline: WATCH: Footwear — source from United Kingdom (emerging origin)
insights.narrative: **Initiate footwear procurement trials from the United Kingdom and Morocco, deliberately avoiding the Vietnam/China/Indonesia origins relied upon by all major competitors.** Demand signals for footwear in Germany show strong acceleration (0.66) with positive momentum and growth, justifying a proactive sourcing move ahead of the curve. Established origins—Vietnam (35% share, –34% growth), China (34% share, –36% growth), and Indonesia (7% share, –43% growth)—are all in steep decline, while the UK and Morocco are identified as emerging alternatives that would deliver clear supply-chain differentiation versus Aldi, Tesco, Carrefour, Auchan, Mercadona, and Walmart, who are uniformly concentrated in those contracting Asian origins.

insights.headline: WATCH: Tea & Matcha — source from Japan (emerging origin)
insights.narrative: **Prioritise sourcing Tea & Matcha from Japan, with Poland as a secondary origin to monitor.** Japan holds the highest origin share (20.8%) among tracked sources and is the only origin showing strong positive growth (+25.8%), confirming it as the standout emerging supply signal for the Netherlands market. Shifting volume toward Japan also creates clear competitive differentiation, as key rivals Rewe and Sainsbury's are anchored to China and India respectively — both of which are in notable decline (-27.9% and -14.4% growth). Poland warrants a secondary watch given modest but positive growth (+9.7%) and a meaningful share (8.6%), offering potential near-market supply flexibility.

insights.headline: WATCH: Audio & Earbuds — source from Mexico (emerging origin)
insights.narrative:
## Procurement Recommendation: Audio & Earbuds

**Increase sourcing allocation toward Mexico for Audio & Earbuds**, where supply-side growth is accelerating strongly (+43.1% growth, emerging origin status), while deliberately reducing dependency on China and Vietnam, which together hold the majority of current supply share but are both in significant volume decline (-13.5% and -18.8% respectively). Demand signals show positive momentum (0.155) with accelerating growth (0.064 acceleration), justifying a proactive sourcing shift rather than a reactive one. Competitors Aldi and Ahold Delhaize remain concentrated in China, meaning a Mexico pivot offers Lidl a meaningful supply chain differentiation advantage while hedging against further contraction from the dominant traditional origins.

insights.headline: WATCH: Beauty & Cosmetics — source from South Korea (emerging origin)
insights.narrative: **Prioritise sourcing Beauty & Cosmetics from South Korea, supplemented by established supply from France and Germany.** South Korea is the standout emerging origin, delivering the highest growth rate across all tracked origins (26.67%) despite already holding a meaningful 16.88% share — and competitor Edeka is already active there, signalling validated commercial viability in this market. France retains the largest share (29.24%) with solid growth (7.49%) and should be maintained as the anchor origin, while Germany (9.49% share, 11.47% growth) offers a reliable near-shore complement that balances supply chain resilience.

insights.headline: WATCH: Candles & Home Fragrance — source from Spain (emerging origin)
insights.narrative: **Initiate procurement trials for Candles & Home Fragrance from Spain, Turkey, South Korea, and Brazil to capitalise on positive demand momentum while reducing dependence on declining incumbent origins.** Wikipedia-tracked demand for this category in Spain shows consistent positive growth (2.14%) and accelerating momentum, yet all current top origins — Vietnam (45.6% share, -3.6% growth), China (-22.3%), Poland (-16.0%), Belgium (-11.2%), and Mexico (-13.3%) — are contracting, signalling a structural sourcing gap. Shifting volume toward the four recommended emerging origins also provides a competitive edge over Carrefour, whose Spain market sourcing is anchored in India, differentiating Lidl's offer on provenance and supply chain resilience.

insights.headline: HOLD: Small Kitchen Appliances — source from Czechia (emerging origin)
insights.narrative: **Maintain current procurement volumes for Small Kitchen Appliances in France, but initiate supplier exploration in Czechia as a future diversification option.** Demand signals are weak — showing negative momentum (-0.0475) and slight decline in growth (-0.0099) — making this the wrong moment to scale up sourcing; however, the positive acceleration (+0.0481) warrants monitoring for a potential turning point. Czechia is flagged as an emerging origin worth developing as a supply alternative to China (which holds a 53.6% share but is declining at -13.9%), particularly given that key competitors Aldi, Edeka, and Walmart are all concentrated in China, creating an opportunity for Lidl to differentiate its supply chain resilience through nearshore European sourcing.

insights.headline: HOLD: Toys & Collectibles — source from Mexico (emerging origin)
insights.narrative: **Maintain current Toys & Collectibles procurement volumes but shift a portion of sourcing toward Mexico, where supply-side growth is running at +23.3% and it is the only origin in the top-five showing positive momentum.** Reduce incremental exposure to China (currently 72.9% share but contracting at -26.1%) and deprioritise Vietnam, Germany, and Indonesia, all of which show similarly negative supply growth. This rebalancing is warranted by weakening demand signals (Wikipedia momentum at -0.38, negative growth and acceleration) that argue against volume expansion, while Mexico's emerging-origin status offers a differentiated, lower-risk supply lane compared to the China-dominant position held by key competitors Tesco, Ahold Delhaize, and Walmart.

insights.headline: HOLD: Pet Care — source from Netherlands (emerging origin)
insights.narrative:
## Procurement Recommendation: Pet Care — Italy

**Hold current procurement volumes for Pet Care in Italy and initiate exploratory sourcing from the Netherlands**, supported by secondary evaluation of Czechia and Poland as complementary emerging origins. Demand signals are flat across all measured dimensions (zero momentum, growth, and acceleration from the single available source), indicating no case for volume expansion at this time, but the supply-side data justifies a strategic origin shift: established origins Germany and Thailand are both in volume decline (-0.92% and -38.84% growth respectively), China is contracting sharply (-53.98%), and competitor Rewe is exposed to Thailand — an origin showing severe deterioration. France (+5.51%) and Belgium (+14.38%) show positive growth and warrant retention in the mix, but the Netherlands is specifically flagged as an emerging recommended origin, offering supply diversification away from the highest-risk corridors. Maintaining stable volumes while repositioning sourcing toward the Netherlands (and monitoring Czechia and Poland) reduces concentration risk and pre-empts further erosion from declining incumbent origins without overcommitting ahead of confirmed demand recovery.
